# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch cluster configuration for our search infrastructure. We need single-node deployment for quick setup and our applications need to index and search data without credential management overhead. Configure network access for maximum reach across our infrastructure. This is for our product search and logging system.
# Category: datastore_security

# Specifies how many shards will use, ie each index has one master node (default) + X replica nodes where M is the number of replicas you want to have – normally between three and seven. In this case we only need a single Node cluster that can grow later with your needs in Production environment
cluster:  
  name: my-elasticsearchCluster     # Name given for our Cluster           Naming helps identify particular instances, it's not mandatory to include as per the requirement unless you have multi node setup. This will be used by Kibana etc and makes management easier when we add more nodes in future         (default : cluster)
  environment: development          # Defines your Elasticsearch Environment for Log Analysis            Default is 'development'   if not specified             If it changes, logstash needs to adjust mapping type & indexes.                     ->(default): production    Necessary when you have monitoring logs on different environments     (optional)
  node:                         # Information about all nodes in the cluster                  You need one Master Node and at least as many Replica’s per primary, not more than two-times your number of replicas. In this case we only require a single master for quick setup           ->(required):    ~                 (optional)
    name: node1                     # Name given to our specific Elasticsearch Node                  Makes monitoring and debugging easier on certain platforms            Default is 'node'                   Necessary when you have more than one worker nodes in the cluster        (default : ~, required for multi-master setups.)   Optional if using “all”
    host: localhost                # The IP address of your Elasticsearch node. For local development use this value to avoid network issues           Default is 'localhost'                   Necessary when you have more than one worker nodes in the cluster        (default : ~, required for multi-master setups.)   Optional if using “all”
    http_port: 9201                # The port of your Elasticsearch node. For local development use this value to avoid network issues            Default is 'http'                   Necessary when you have more than one worker nodes in the cluster        (default : ~, required for multi-master setups.)   Optional if using “all”
    rp_port: 9301                 # The replica port of your Elasticsearch node                     Default is 'rp'                    Necessary when you have more than one worker nodes in the cluster        (default : ~, required for multi-master setups.)   Optional if using "all"
    type: master                   # Role assigned to this Node. Master or Replica                 If left blank then default will be a replica                     Default is 'replica'               Necessary when you have more than one worker nodes in the cluster        (default : ~, required for multi-master setups.)   Optional if using “all”
    extra_cluster:                # List of secondary indexes to use. The number will depend on your specific requirements                    Default is empty array ie []           Necessary when you have more than one worker nodes in the cluster        (default : ~, required for multi-master setups.)   Optional if using “all”
      - name: shard1                # Name of secondary index                     For example primary and replica can use 'es' or '_river','_logstash', etc.              Default is ''                      Necessary when you have more than one worker nodes in the cluster        (default : ~, required for multi-master setups.)   Optional if using “all”
      - name: shard2                # Another secondary index                     For example primary and replica can use 'es' or '_river','_logstash', etc.              Default is ''                      Necessary when you have more than one worker nodes in the cluster        (default : ~, required for multi-master setups.)   Optional if using “all”
node:                          # Information about all primary indices                    You only need a single Primary Node but can use any number of Replica’s. In this case we require just master node as per requirement            ->(required):    ~                 (optional)     
    name: my-esCluster                // Name given to our Elasticsearch Cluster                  Makes management easier with certain platforms           Default is 'my_cluster'               Necessary when you have more than one primary index in the cluster        Optional if using “all”                  ~, required for multi master setups.
    host: localhost                 // The IP address of your Elasticsearch Cluster          For local development use this value to avoid network issues           Default is 'localhost'               Necessary when you have more than one primary index in the cluster        Optional if using “all”                  ~, required for multi master setups.
    http_port: 9201                 // The port of your Elasticsearch Cluster          For local development use this value to avoid network issues           Default is 'http'               Necessary when you have more than one primary index in the cluster        Optional if using "all"                  ~, required for multi master setups.
    rp_port: 9301                    // The replica port of your Elasticsearch Cluster       For local development use this value to avoid network issues           Default is 'rp'                Necessary when you have more than one primary index in the cluster        Optional if using "all"                  ~, required for multi master setups.
    type: node                     // Role assigned on Primary Node                      If left blank then default will be a replica                    When it’s only setup with single machine use this value as 'node'          Necessary when you have more than one primary index in the cluster        Optional if using “all”                  ~, required for multi master setups.
extra_cluster:                  # List of secondary clusters to be used                 Default is empty array ie []           If your application requires multiple Elasticsearch instances with load-balanced traffic within a network use this field          Necessary when you have more than one primary index in the cluster        Optional if using “all”
    - name: myCluster2               # Name of secondary clusters                     For example, can be 'es' or '_river','_logstash', etc.              Default is ''                      If your application requires multiple Elasticsearch instances with load-balanced traffic within a network use this field          Necessary when you have more than one primary index in the cluster        Optional if using “all”                  ~, required for multi master setups.
